vulnerability.title: Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval
vulnerability.description: The maxpoll should be configured to in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) to continuously poll time servers. To configure maxpoll in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) add the following after each server, pool or peer entry: maxpoll to server directives. If using chrony, any pool directives should be configured too.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( rpm --quiet -q chrony || rpm --quiet -q ntp ); }; then var_time_service_set_maxpoll='' pof="/usr/sbin/pidof" CONFIG_FILES="/etc/ntp.conf" $pof ntpd || { CHRONY_D_PATH=/etc/chrony.d/ mapfile -t CONFIG_FILES < <(find ${CHRONY_D_PATH}.* -type f -name '*.conf') CONFIG_FILES+=(/etc/chrony.conf) } # get list of ntp files for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Set maxpoll values to var_time_service_set_maxpoll sed -i "s/^\(\(server\|pool\|peer\).*maxpoll\) [0-9,-][0-9]*\(.*\)$/\1 $var_time_service_set_maxpoll \3/" "$config_file" done for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Add maxpoll to server, pool or peer entries without maxpoll grep "^\(server\|pool\|peer\)" "$config_file" | grep -v maxpo

triage_decision.reason: The finding is related to configuring the time service maxpoll interval, which is a low-risk change that is unlikely to lock out access.

commands_executed: rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( rpm --quiet -q chrony || rpm --quiet -q ntp ); }

var_time_service_set_maxpoll='10' pof="/usr/sbin/pidof" CONFIG_FILES="/etc/ntp.conf" $pof ntpd || { CHRONY_D_PATH="/etc/chrony.d/" mapfile -t CONFIG_FILES < <(find ${CHRONY_D_PATH} -type f -name '*.conf') CONFIG_FILES+=(/etc/chrony.conf) }

llm_verdict.message: The agent failed to remediate the OpenSCAP finding "Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval" due to connection timeout issues. The agent was unable to modify the `/etc/chrony.conf` or `/etc/ntp.conf` files, and the post-remediation scan still failed.

review_feedback: The remediation attempt failed to modify the configuration files; all applied commands returned exit_code 255, leaving /etc/chrony.conf and /etc/ntp.conf unchanged and the post‑remediation scan still fails. Suggestions: Check existence of /etc/ntp.conf and /etc/chrony.conf (or files in /etc/chrony.d/) before applying changes; Use robust regex that matches server/pool/peer lines optionally containing maxpoll and replaces or appends maxpoll 10 safely; Apply changes idempotently and verify success with a post‑change grep; Add error handling and rollback to original configuration if modification fails; Test the script in a staging environment before production deployment

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Remediation attempt incomplete; failed to properly set maxpoll values, resulting in scan failures.
concerns: Script contains syntax errors and incomplete handling of configuration files
Commands terminated with exit_code 255 indicating failure
maxpoll directive not actually added to NTP or chrony configurations
Potential issues with regex and quoting in sed replacements
Lack of validation that intended files exist before modification
suggested_improvements: Validate presence of NTP/chrony configuration files before applying changes
Use robust regex patterns that match server, pool, or peer lines with optional existing maxpoll entries
Apply changes idempotently and verify success with post‑change checks
Include error handling and rollback mechanisms
Test modifications on a staging system before production deployment

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation attempt failed to modify the configuration files; all applied commands returned exit_code 255, leaving /etc/chrony.conf and /etc/ntp.conf unchanged and the post‑remediation scan still fails.
concerns: Commands terminated with exit_code 255, indicating failure to write files
No validation that target files exist before attempting sed modifications
Regex pattern incomplete and may not match existing maxpoll entries or all server/pool/peer lines
Hard‑coded maxpoll value without configurable justification
Creation of /etc/chrony.d/ and appending to unrelated files without checking file type or permissions
Lack of idempotent logic and verification that maxpoll was actually added
suggested_improvements: Check existence of /etc/ntp.conf and /etc/chrony.conf (or files in /etc/chrony.d/) before applying changes
Use robust regex that matches server/pool/peer lines optionally containing maxpoll and replaces or appends maxpoll 10 safely
Apply changes idempotently and verify success with a post‑change grep
Add error handling and rollback to original configuration if modification fails
Test the script in a staging environment before production deployment
